status_model: Constitution §8 work-order states + capability-map markers (queued, delivered) + ledger statuses (open, resolved, archived). Target-tool status mapping lives in skills/pm-export/SKILL.md.

items.title: BOOTSTRAP
items.body: Repo foundation: north-star artifacts + work-order machinery (brownfield v4). Realized by WO-0001.

items.title: Artifact templates
items.body: Charter, constitution, domain-model, contracts, capability-map, feature, ADR, ledger templates — each with a governance header + authoring instructions. Planned work order: WO-0002 (not yet cut).

items.title: Schemas
items.body: Feature and capability-map frontmatter schemas. Planned work order: WO-0003 (not yet cut).

items.title: Playbooks & commands
items.body: Intake, specification, implementation, verification/repair, and change-order playbooks & commands. Planned work order: WO-0004 (not yet cut).

items.title: README & design notes
items.body: Full README rewrite + lineage essay (stepwise refinement, AV/construction work orders, Ansible, CI/CD). Planned work order: WO-0005 (not yet cut).

items.title: Worked example
items.body: Candidate: promote this bootstrap's own artifacts to /examples/. Planned work order: WO-0006 (not yet cut).

items.title: Adapter shims
items.body: CLAUDE.md + Copilot instruction templates; pointers only, one screen max. Planned work order: WO-0007 (not yet cut).

items.title: Common rules
items.body: 6 always-apply rule files in rules/common/. Delivered outside the work-order system (Ledger L-011).

items.title: Language rule stubs
items.body: TypeScript, Python, Go rule stubs in rules/<lang>/. Delivered outside the work-order system (Ledger L-011); stubs unpopulated (L-013).

items.title: Development agents
items.body: 5 subagent definitions in agents/: architect, code-reviewer, security-reviewer, spec-miner, silent-failure-hunter. Delivered outside the work-order system (Ledger L-011).

items.title: Governance agents
items.body: 3 subagent definitions in agents/: planner, builder, gatekeeper. Delivered outside the work-order system (Ledger L-011).

items.title: Skills
items.body: 7 invokable skills in skills/ (initial batch): ADR, multi-agent exploration, project philosophy, work-order intake, deep research, code review, session handoff. Delivered outside the work-order system (Ledger L-011).

items.title: Installation profiles
items.body: 3 manifests in profiles/: Minimal, Standard, Full. Delivered outside the work-order system (Ledger L-011).

items.title: Agent + skill schemas
items.body: schemas/agent.schema.json, schemas/skill.schema.json. Delivered outside the work-order system (Ledger L-011).

items.title: Validation playbook + WO clarifications
items.body: playbooks/validation-checklist.md closeout checklist + clarifications frontmatter field on templates/work-order.md (v0.3.0). Delivered by lightweight path (Ledger L-014).

items.title: PM export
items.body: pm-export skill + schemas/pm-export.schema.json + derived pm/pm-export.json — tool-agnostic projection of work orders, features, and ledger for external trackers. Delivered by lightweight path (Ledger L-015).

items.title: Foundation — Boresight v4 bootstrap; north-star artifacts + work-order machinery
items.body: Establishes Boresight as v4 of the AI_Agent_Dev_Docs repository — north-star artifacts, work-order machinery, and reconciliation of existing v3 content into a companion layer. On completion, every subsequent unit of work can be specified, executed, and verified using the system's own machinery. Status: review — awaiting AC-12 (human ratification of charter + constitution) before review → complete. Spawns WO-0002…WO-0007 (queued, not yet cut).

items.title: Bootstrap paradox
items.body: **Bootstrap paradox.** WO-0001 predates the schema and capability map that govern it. Resolved: AC-3 passed retroactively (WO-0001 frontmatter validates against `schemas/work-order.schema.json`; evidence in `/work-orders/WO-0001.md`).

items.title: Project name
items.body: **Project name.** "Boresight" ratified 2026-06-12 as production codename only; codename-neutrality constraint (Constitution §1.3) binds. Public release name deferred. Candidates: Keel, Jig, codejig — also candidates as module names.

items.title: Status derivation
items.body: **Status derivation.** Generated vs. hand-maintained capability map deferred past v0.1. Hand-maintained for now; status canon (Constitution §3) still binds.

items.title: Model-tier routing config format
items.body: **Model-tier routing config format.** Tier names stay abstract in v0.1; concrete model pins live in harness adapters, never in work orders.

items.title: Role-filtered "compiled" context views
items.body: **Role-filtered "compiled" context views.** Deferred; section structure serves as the role filter for now.

items.title: Adapter auto-generation (`factory sync`)
items.body: **Adapter auto-generation (`factory sync`).** Deferred to v0.2; WO-0007 ships hand-authored shim templates.

items.title: Gatekeeper validator deferred to a later maturity tier, against two peer-review recommendations (htf-coder §4, codex §2.7) — deliberate decision 2026-06-12. v0.1 schema validation is a one-off manual run recorded in evidence blocks. Revisit when work-order volume or parallel work justifies standing automation.
items.body: **Gatekeeper validator deferred** to a later maturity tier, against two peer-review recommendations (htf-coder §4, codex §2.7) — deliberate decision 2026-06-12. v0.1 schema validation is a one-off manual run recorded in evidence blocks. Revisit when work-order volume or parallel work justifies standing automation.

items.title: v3 session-layer integration
items.body: **v3 session-layer integration.** CONTEXT/PROGRESS/HANDOFF/WORKFLOW remain a companion layer. Full integration (run-records, playbook conversion, `/templates` reorg) deferred to a later development stage; until then `/templates` is shared (Constitution §4.2).

items.title: Deferred template features per codex review: Semantic Versioning of Intent, cost-estimation fields, specialized-reviewer routing (`review_requirements`), hermeticity constraints beyond the `infra_block` trigger. Revisit with real run-record data.
items.body: **Deferred template features** per codex review: Semantic Versioning of Intent, cost-estimation fields, specialized-reviewer routing (`review_requirements`), hermeticity constraints beyond the `infra_block` trigger. Revisit with real run-record data.

items.title: New artifact classes (Rule, Skill, Agent, Profile) pending charter enumeration
items.body: **New artifact classes (Rule, Skill, Agent, Profile) pending charter enumeration.** The charter's fixed artifact-class list does not include these four classes (created 2026-06-16 as part of the library layer). Governance headers for these artifacts use nearest-fit existing classes with a parenthetical note. Charter amendment via change order required to make these classes official. Blocked on AC-12 (WO-0001 human gate) before charter can be amended.

items.title: Work-order coverage gap for library layer (F-008–F-013)
items.body: **Work-order coverage gap for library layer (F-008–F-013).** The promptware library layer (rules, agents, skills, profiles, schemas) was created without governing work orders. Constitution §8 requires work orders for governed artifacts. Gap noted; options: (a) retroactive work orders, (b) change order formalizing a "lightweight path" for library additions. Revisit when library stabilizes.

items.title: Model pinning in agent frontmatter
items.body: **Model pinning in agent frontmatter.** Agent definitions in `agents/` intentionally omit `model:` field, consistent with L-004 (model pins belong in harness adapters). The `model` field is available in `schemas/agent.schema.json` for downstream consumers to populate. Revisit if Claude Code's default model resolution behavior changes.

items.title: Language rule stubs are unpopulated
items.body: **Language rule stubs are unpopulated.** `rules/typescript/README.md`, `rules/python/README.md`, `rules/go/README.md` are valid stubs but contain placeholder sections rather than authoritative conventions. Each downstream project should extend them with project-specific content. A future work order should decide whether kst_promptware ships opinionated defaults or only structure for these stubs.

items.title: Validation playbook + WO clarifications field delivered without a work order
items.body: **Validation playbook + WO clarifications field delivered without a work order.** `playbooks/validation-checklist.md` (merging `references/enhancement-proposal-062126.md` proposals #2 + #5) and a `clarifications` frontmatter field on `templates/work-order.md` (frontmatter-only variant of proposal #1 — no state-machine or constitution change) were delivered 2026-06-21 by direct human authorization, consistent with the L-011 lightweight-path precedent; no work order or change order was cut. `templates/work-order.md` bumped v0.2.0 → v0.3.0; `schemas/work-order.schema.json` gained an optional `clarifications` property. This formalizes but does not resolve L-007 — the checklist is the structured manual step L-007 anticipated; standing automation is still deferred. Proposals #3 (convergence assessment), #4 (adapter shims), and #6 (override hierarchy) from the same enhancement proposal were deliberately held back.

items.title: Tool-agnostic PM export delivered without a work order
items.body: **Tool-agnostic PM export delivered without a work order.** The `pm-export` skill (`skills/pm-export/SKILL.md`), its output contract (`schemas/pm-export.schema.json`), and the derived export (`pm/pm-export.json` + `pm/README.md`) were delivered 2026-07-15 by direct human authorization, consistent with the L-011 / L-014 lightweight-path precedent; no work order or change order was cut. The export is a *derived* projection of `work-orders/`, `capability-map.md`, and `ledger.md` into a tool-neutral issue model — docs stay canonical (§3), regeneration replaces it, and the only state it holds beyond the docs is `external_ids` (per-tool sync ids). First concrete sync target is GitHub Issues; the neutral core is unchanged for Linear or Jira. Artifact-class note: `pm/pm-export.json` and `pm/README.md` use nearest-fit classes (derived index) pending the same charter-enumeration path as the Rule/Skill/Agent/Profile classes (L-010). The skill ships `maturity: experimental`. Capability map gains F-016.
